articles.title: Kolkata weather today (April 5): Will the 'City of Joy' get a respite from scorching heat? Here's what the - The Economic Times
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") West Bengal
articles.full_text:
The Met office has predicted cloudy days ahead, with chances of thunderstorms striking the city over the next five days. The IMD on Sunday forecast thunderstorms in some districts of West Bengal till April 9, which would bring down maximum temperatures by a few notches.

Kolkata weather today
The weather in Kolkata could remain partly cloudy on Sunday and Monday and the city and its surrounding districts could be lashed by ‘intense thunderstorms’ between Tuesday and Thursday. Conditions are set to turn favourable for thunderstorm activity after a scorching spell, said the Regional Meteorological Centre on Saturday.ALSO READ: Gujarat weather forecast
Kolkata rain update
Kolkata is set for a stormy and rain-soaked week starting April 6, with frequent thunderstorms, lightning strikes, and gusty winds expected to impact daily life across the city.On April 8, Kolkata will continue to experience rain and thundershowers, keeping temperatures lower between 22°C and 31°C. Lightning strikes and strong winds of 40–50 kmph remain a concern.
Similar conditions will persist on April 9, with thunderstorms and rain expected throughout the day. The minimum and maximum temperatures will stay around 22°C and 31°C, respectively, along with lightning and gusty winds up to 50 kmph.
By April 10, weather conditions may slightly improve, with a partly cloudy sky and chances of thunderstorm development. Temperatures are expected to range between 25°C and 33°C, and no major weather warning has been issued for the day. Kolkata residents should prepare for a stormy week with frequent rain, thunderstorms, lightning and strong winds, especially between April 7 and April 9.
“We could see a slow drop in temperature as a cloud cover starts appearing from Sunday. It could lead to sporadic rain in and around Kolkata. This will be due to localised conditions — clouds triggered by heating and moisture incursion, which is a common phenomenon in summer. But chances of more severe thunderstorms will increase over the subsequent three days,” Sourish Bandyopadhyay, weather scientist at RMC, told Times of India.
“We expect a trough and a cyclonic circulation to form near Bengal that will lead to clouds and thunderstorms. There will also be an indirect impact of a western disturbance. It will raise the chances of thunderstorms in Kolkata and across south Bengal,” added Bandyopadhyay.
Bengal weather news
The IMD said that thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds reaching speeds of 40 to 50 kmph are likely over Purulia, Bankura, Purba and Paschim Bardhaman, Paschim Medinipur, Jhargram, Murshidabad, North 24 Parganas and Hooghly districts.Light to moderate rain or thundershowers are likely over the rest of the districts of south Bengal, the weather office said.
Thunderstorms, lightning and gusty wind reaching speed of 40 to 50 kmph are likely over the sub-Himalayan districts of Darjeeling, Kalimpong, Alipurduar, Jalpaiguri and Cooch Behar, it said.
Light to moderate rain or thundershowers are likely over the remaining districts of north Bengal till April 9, it said
(With TOI inputs)
